Hey guys, JB here, daily motivation time, skills training time. Today it's motivation. Listen guys, I wanna talk about something that's really a core principle of mine or something just drives me crazy, right? It's people that make excuses or you just making excuses for yourself like mothers. Let me just tell you something, right? You know in your heart when you're being the absolute best that you can be. And you also know when you're not doing that. You also know when you're not working as hard as you can, when you're really selling yourself short, when you're quitting early, you're not putting into preparation. Let me tell you, you're f*****g kidding, nobody but yourself. I'm dead serious. You know, it's time at a certain point in time. You know what being an adult is on some level, on many levels. It's about not having to have someone looking over your shoulder to try to ensure that you actually protect your own life. You set yourself up for greatness, that you make your life everything it should be. When you're younger, your parents, they're always saying, you know, oh please, you know, study hard and I wanna help you keep your options open for the future and make sure you don't cut options off. That's what being a child is about. When you're a child, you don't have the emotional wear of the strength, the muscle emotionally to do the smart thing for yourself. So if you're lucky, you have parents who watch out for you. They look after you and they force you to do the right thing even when you don't want to. And you might be pissed at them if you could resent them, but at the end of the day, you'll thank them because by doing that, they set you up for greatness. But now that you're an adult, now that you're in the real world, let me just tell you something, that doesn't work anymore. You have to be your own vigilante for success. If you think that you're gonna be able to rely on someone else whispering in your ear, other than me, I'm the only person you got. Okay, this is what I do. All right, that's gonna help you. It's gonna guide you. But I gotta empower you by telling you the truth sometimes, which is meaning like, dude, it's up to you. If you can get yourself to face the facts that it's within your control, that it's the moves that you make, the decisions you make, the abilities that you have right now to force yourself to essentially harness the power you have to focus on what you want to do, where you want to go, and to force yourself to put in the time, the energy, essentially put in the preparation that's necessary to achieve master's success. If you can't get yourself to that, then honestly, you're gonna be broke the rest of your life. You seriously, you will. There's nothing I can say, there's nothing I can do to protect you from yourself if you have that mentality that like, you know, half measures are okay. Maybe you know, because I'm not really in the mood today, right? I don't really feel like working that hard. I'll still be okay. No, you won't be okay. Let me tell you something, the people who get ahead in life, the people who soar to success the people who really end up living a first-class life, you know what the difference between them and other people? They don't make excuses. They don't put a half of their effort into anything. When they do something, they go at it full tilt. They put every last bit of themselves into it. There are no like, you know, let me, you know, I'm tired today. So like, you know, I did my best. Losers always whine about doing their best. Winners go home and fuck the prom queen. I heard that in the, by the way, the movie The Rock, great line, right? Winners go home and fuck the prom queen. And here's the point, okay? Bottom line is that the loser's mentality is like, um, I really tried hard. I did my best. That is not enough. Your best is not enough. It's really not. I'll tell you what is enough. What's enough is that at the end of the day, you can look yourself in the mirror. At the end of the day and say, you know what? I wouldn't have done anything differently because of my work ethic, the amount of energy I expended. I did what I needed to do. I'm not saying you want to change certain actions you took. That's a different story. Okay. You're going to execute it better, but you don't look at your life on that for that day and say, you know what? Oh God, I really, you know, I was really kind of going at things haphardly. I really was tired. I was lackluster. If you, you have to cut that out. If you want to live a first class life, let me just give you a reality check, man. You got to start training yourself to be the best you can be every single day. And that means stop making excuses. Stop thinking it's okay to be your best 50% of the time. It's not. You got to show up at the top of your game every single day. Cause let me tell you, the rest of the winners are, they should, they'll have their good days. They have their bad days because they show up fully invested, fully engaged, fully into it every single day. And they win their wins and losses, right? Their days where they're more on or more off, but their energy was into it. Their emotions was into it. They were there. That's what I'm talking about here. It's a decision you make and that's what it is. It's a decision. Because this is not like some sort of skill. Like, I'm going to teach you how to give it your all. You can't teach someone to give it your all. It's got to be a matter of pride. It's your pride. Say, for me, I could not imagine being thought of as a human being that went through life and didn't give their all to everything they did. I could not go through, I could not feel okay about that. There's no strategy. It's just about who are you? What are you capable of? Stop thinking that it's okay to just go at things halfway or even 75% of the way. I want 120% of the way. Seriously, when you're going at life, just go at it. Go at it with reckless abandon. That doesn't mean not using the right strategies. It doesn't mean being unethical. I'm talking about going at things and just rar, attacking each morning. Get to the habit of doing that and watch what happens to your life. It's like anything else. It's like a muscle. You build muscle with this stuff. You get yourself into the habit of just going out and being a force of nature. I'm a force of nature. You know what? You, every last one of you, you're all forces of nature whether you know it or not. You are. You have the ability to turn that shit on at a moment's notice and be whatever you want to be, to design whatever life you want. Anything less is just a story. Conditioning, bad programming from when you were growing up, when your parents conditioned you for mediocrity and averageism. Average sucks. You know that. How many times have I said to you that what stops people from achieving, really achieving in life is not that they set their goals too high and they miss them. That's not what it is. Because people set their two goals too low and they hit them. The enemy of great is average. Because when you average, there's no pain. And when there's no pain, no discomfort, people don't really change. Average sucks moose cock. It's my thoughts for today. And I know I'm right. You know why? Because I see it in my own life. The way I live, the way I do it, I go through, I make my mistakes. I win. I have my losses. But let me tell you, I go out in life and look how extraordinary my life has been. You all be the same way. You know, people get a movie made about you, but you'll be a force. You'll be someone that when you enter a room, when you move through life, there's freaking ripples. Don't let yourself settle for anything less. That's a gift you give yourself. I love you all. We'll talk again tomorrow. Kick ass today, right? Kick some serious ass.
